zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Hussey於1355年出生在英國英格蘭爾時，她的父親William Hussey31歲，母親Mary Elizabeth De Verdun29歲。她於1372年與Ralph de la Brookes在英國英格蘭結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。她去世於1425年在英國英格蘭爾麗頓 白 內斯頓享年70歲。

nameMeanings.name: Hussey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: of Norman origin, a nickname for someone who habitually wore a distinctive pair of boots or gaiters, from Old French hosed, hoset, housé, Middle English hosey, hus(s)y,‘booted, gaitered’ (from Latin hosatus).

English: status name or nickname from Middle English hus(e)wyf ‘mistress of a family; wife of a householder’ (a compound of Old English hūs ‘house’ + wīf ‘woman’). Though originally a woman's name, it is often found as a male surname, presumably in a derogatory sense. The vocabulary word became hussie, with the meaning ‘disreputable woman’, in the 16th century; the surname, however, is not associated with this meaning.
Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó hEodhusa ‘descendant of Eodhus’. This was the name of a bardic family associated with the Maguires of Fermanagh, also Anglicized as Oswell or Oswald .

variants.name: Hosea
meanings: English: variant of   Hussey  . Similarity with the Old Testament prophet's name Hosea (see 3 below) is accidental.
English: in Scotland, where the name is more common, it is sometimes a variant of   Hosie  , itself a form of   Hussey  , or else a variant of   Hossack  .
African (mainly Nigeria and Tanzania): from the Biblical personal name Hosea. In the Old Testament, Hosea was a prophet who married a prostitute in order to illustrate Israel's unfaithfulness to God.

variants.name: Hosie
meanings: Scottish (northeast Scotland): variant of Irish   Hussey  .

variants.name: Oswell
meanings: Irish (Ulster): adopted as an English equivalent of Gaelic Ó hEodhusa ‘descendant of Eodhus’ (see Hussey ).

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Hussey was born in 1355, in Cheshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, William Hussey, was 31 and her mother, Mary Elizabeth De Verdun, was 29. She married Ralph de la Brookes in 1372, in England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. She died in 1425, in Leighton by Neston, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 70.
